mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations yield negative forward returns in more than 60 percent of cases across multiple recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher rates, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 5-day returns in more than 50 percent of tightening periods over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth signals strengthening corporate earnings fundamentals, which mechanically lifts S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above threshold consistently precede positive 20-day returns in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting equity valuations through higher expected earnings.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative forward returns in more than 55 percent of observations over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales during a late-cycle expansion signal peak consumer exhaustion and impending earnings revisions, causing negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines in late-cycle periods are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 60 percent of cases.
